001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
024
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
029
030/**
031 * Java bean wrapper class.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
034 *
035 * A wrapper that wraps Java bean instances inside of a {@link Map} interface that allows properties on the wrapped
036 * object can be accessed using the {@link Map#get(Object) get()} and {@link Map#put(Object,Object) put()} methods.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Create instances through the bean API.  For the bean-modeling-only path use {@link BeanMap#of(Object)} or
040 * {@link BeanMap#of(Object, BeanMeta)}; the marshalling layer's {@code MarshallingSession#toBeanMap(Object)}
041 * additionally wires a {@link BeanSession} into the resulting map for session-aware behavior.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean property order</h5>
044 *
045 * The order of the properties returned by the {@link Map#keySet() keySet()} and {@link Map#entrySet() entrySet()}
046 * methods is as follows:
047 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
048 *    <li>
049 *       If the {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotation specifies an explicit property list via
050 *       {@link BeanType#properties() properties()} (or its synonym {@link BeanType#p() p()}),
051 *       the order matches the annotation.
052 *    <li>
053 *       Otherwise, if {@link BeanType#unsorted() @BeanType(unsorted=true)} is set on the class, or
054 *       {@link BeanConfigContext#isUnsortedProperties()} is enabled on the session, properties are returned
055 *       in their natural reflection order.
056 *    <li>
057 *       Otherwise, properties are returned in alphabetical order.
058 * </ul>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='topic'>POJO swaps</h5>
061 *
062 * If {@code ObjectSwap} transforms are defined on the class types of the properties of this bean or the bean properties
063 * themselves, the {@link #get(Object)} and {@link #put(String, Object)} methods will automatically transform the
064 * property value to and from the serialized form.
065 *
066 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
067 *
068 * Instances are not thread-safe.
069 * This type is mutable (wrapped bean reference, property caches, and optional session pointer) and does not perform
070 * internal synchronization.
071 *
072 *
073 * @param <T> Specifies the type of object that this map encapsulates.
074 */
075@SuppressWarnings({
076   "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean property iteration and filtering
077})
078public class BeanMap<T> extends AbstractMap<String,Object> implements Delegate<T> {
079
080   /**
081    * Convenience method for wrapping a bean inside a {@link BeanMap}.
082    *
083    * @param <T> The bean type.
084    * @param bean The bean being wrapped.
085    * @return A new {@link BeanMap} instance wrapping the bean.
086    */
087   @SuppressWarnings({
088      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: map parameterization is verified at construction.
089   })
090   public static <T> BeanMap<T> of(T bean) {
091      return new BeanMap<>(bean, BeanMeta.of((Class<T>) bean.getClass()));
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Convenience method for wrapping a bean inside a {@link BeanMap} using a pre-built {@link BeanMeta}.
096    *
097    * <p>
098    * This is the bean-modeling entry point — it builds a {@link BeanMap} without going through a
099    * marshalling session, paired with a {@link BeanMeta} typically produced by
100    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}.  No marshalling session is attached.
101    *
102    * @param <T> The bean type.
103    * @param bean The bean being wrapped.
104    * @param meta The bean metadata.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
105    * @return A new {@link BeanMap} instance wrapping the bean.
106    */
107   public static <T> BeanMap<T> of(T bean, BeanMeta<T> meta) {
108      return new BeanMap<>(bean, meta);
109   }
110
111   /** The wrapped object. */
112   protected T bean;
113
114   /** Temporary holding cache for beans with read-only properties.  Normally null. */
115   protected Map<String,Object> propertyCache;
116
117   /** Temporary holding cache for bean properties of array types when the add() method is being used. */
118   protected Map<String,List<?>> arrayPropertyCache;
119
120   /** The BeanMeta associated with the class of the object. */
121   protected BeanMeta<T> meta;
122   private BeanSession session;
123
124   private final String typePropertyName;
125
126   /**
127    * Constructor.
128    *
129    * <p>
130    * Bean-modeling-only constructor. Does not carry a {@link BeanSession} reference.
131    * The marshalling layer wires the session in via {@link #setBeanSession(BeanSession)}
132    * immediately after construction.
133    *
134    * @param bean The bean to wrap inside this map.
135    * @param meta The metadata associated with the bean class.
136    */
137   public BeanMap(T bean, BeanMeta<T> meta) {
138      this.bean = bean;
139      this.meta = meta;
140      if (ine(meta.getConstructorArgs()))
141         propertyCache = new TreeMap<>();
142      this.typePropertyName = meta.getTypePropertyName();
143   }
144
145   /**
146    * Wires this bean map to a {@link BeanSession}.
147    *
148    * <p>
149    * Called by marshalling-side construction paths (for example,
150    * {@code MarshallingSession#toBeanMap(Object)}) to wire in the session used by session-aware
151    * operations (type conversion, child collection construction, and parser/serializer-backed
152    * conversions) after bean-modeling construction.
153    *
154    * @param value The bean session that produced this bean map.  Typically a marshalling-session implementation.
155    */
156   public void setBeanSession(BeanSession value) {
157      session = value;
158   }
159
160   /**
161    * Add a value to a collection or array property.
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * As a general rule, adding to arrays is not recommended since the array must be recreate each time this method is
165    * called.
166    *
167    * @param property Property name or child-element name (if {@code @Xml(childName)} is specified).
168    * @param value The value to add to the collection or array.
169    */
170   public void add(String property, Object value) {
171      var p = getPropertyMeta(property);
172      if (p == null) {
173         if (meta.getConfig().isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties())
174            return;
175         throw brex(meta.getClassInfo(), "Bean property ''{0}'' not found.", property);
176      }
177      p.add(this, property, value);
178   }
179
180   @Override /* Overridden from Map */
181   public boolean containsKey(Object property) {
182      // JUNEAU-248: Match the behavior of keySet() - only check properties map, not hiddenProperties
183      var key = emptyIfNull(property);
184      if (meta.getProperties().containsKey(key) && ! "*".equals(key))
185         return true;
186      if (nn(meta.getDynaProperty())) {
187         try {
188            return meta.getDynaProperty().getDynaMap(bean).containsKey(key);
189         } catch (Exception e) {
190            throw brex(e);
191         }
192      }
193      return false;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Returns all the properties associated with the bean.
198    *
199    * @return A new set.
200    */
201   @Override
202   public Set<Entry<String,Object>> entrySet() {
203
204      // If this bean has a dyna-property, then we need to construct the entire set before returning.
205      // Otherwise, we can create an iterator without a new data structure.
206      if (nn(meta.getDynaProperty())) {
207         Set<Entry<String,Object>> s = set();
208         forEachProperty(x -> true, x -> {
209            if (x.isDyna()) {
210               try {
211                  x.getDynaMap(bean).entrySet().forEach(y -> s.add(new BeanMapEntry(this, x, y.getKey())));
212               } catch (Exception e) {
213                  throw brex(e);
214               }
215            } else {
216               s.add(new BeanMapEntry(this, x, x.getName()));
217            }
218         });
219         return s;
220      }
221
222      // Construct our own anonymous set to implement this function.
223      return new AbstractSet<>() {
224
225         // Get the list of properties from the meta object.
226         // Note that the HashMap.values() method caches results, so this collection
227         // will really only be constructed once per bean type since the underlying
228         // map never changes.
229         final Collection<BeanPropertyMeta> pSet = getProperties();
230
231         @Override /* Overridden from Set */
232         public Iterator<Map.Entry<String,Object>> iterator() {
233
234            // Construct our own anonymous iterator that uses iterators against the meta.getProperties()
235            // map to maintain position.  This prevents us from having to construct any of our own
236            // collection objects.
237            return new Iterator<>() {
238
239               final Iterator<BeanPropertyMeta> pIterator = pSet.iterator();
240
241               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
242               public boolean hasNext() {
243                  return pIterator.hasNext();
244               }
245
246               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
247               public Map.Entry<String,Object> next() {
248                  return new BeanMapEntry(BeanMap.this, pIterator.next(), null);
249               }
250
251               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
252               public void remove() {
253                  throw uoex("Cannot remove item from iterator.");
254               }
255            };
256         }
257
258         @Override /* Overridden from Set */
259         public int size() {
260            return pSet.size();
261         }
262      };
263   }
264
265   /**
266    * Performs an action on each property in this bean map.
267    *
268    * <p>
269    * If a {@link BeanSession} is attached and has an active view, only properties that are visible under
270    * that view (as determined by {@link BeanSession#isPropertyInActiveView(BeanPropertyMeta)}) are visited.
271    *
272    * @param filter The filter to apply to properties.
273    * @param action The action.
274    * @return This object.
275    */
276   public BeanMap<T> forEachProperty(Predicate<BeanPropertyMeta> filter, Consumer<BeanPropertyMeta> action) {
277      meta.getProperties().values().stream().filter(filter).filter(this::isInActiveView).forEach(action);
278      return this;
279   }
280
281   /**
282    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the property is visible under the active view on this bean map's session.
283    *
284    * <p>
285    * When no session is attached, or no active view is set, all properties are visible.
286    *
287    * @param pMeta The property to test.
288    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the property is in-view.
289    */
290   protected boolean isInActiveView(BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) {
291      return session == null || session.isPropertyInActiveView(pMeta);
292   }
293
294   /**
295    * Invokes all the getters on this bean and consumes the results.
296    *
297    * @param valueFilter Filter to apply to value before applying action.
298    * @param action The action to perform.
299    * @return The list of all bean property values.
300    */
301   @SuppressWarnings({
302      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean property filtering with predicate
303   })
304   public BeanMap<T> forEachValue(Predicate<Object> valueFilter, BeanPropertyConsumer action) {
305
306      // Normal bean.
307      if (meta.getDynaProperty() == null) {
308         forEachProperty(BeanPropertyMeta::canRead, bpm -> {
309            try {
310               var val = bpm.get(this, null);
311               if (valueFilter.test(val))
312                  action.apply(bpm, bpm.getName(), val, null);
313            } catch (Exception t) {
314               action.apply(bpm, bpm.getName(), null, t);
315            }
316         });
317
318         // Bean with dyna properties.
319      } else {
320         Map<String,BeanPropertyValue> actions = (meta.isUnsortedProperties() ? map() : sortedMap());
321
322         forEachProperty(x -> ! x.isDyna(), bpm -> {
323            try {
324               actions.put(bpm.getName(), new BeanPropertyValue(bpm, bpm.getName(), bpm.get(this, null), null));
325            } catch (Exception t) {
326               actions.put(bpm.getName(), new BeanPropertyValue(bpm, bpm.getName(), null, t));
327            }
328         });
329
330         forEachProperty(BeanPropertyMeta::isDyna, bpm -> {
331            Map<String,Object> dynaMap;
332            try {
333               dynaMap = bpm.getDynaMap(bean);
334            } catch (Exception e) {
335               actions.put(bpm.getName(), new BeanPropertyValue(bpm, bpm.getName(), null, e));
336               return;
337            }
338            if (nn(dynaMap)) {
339               dynaMap.forEach((k, v) -> {
340                  try {
341                     var val = bpm.get(this, k);
342                     actions.put(k, new BeanPropertyValue(bpm, k, val, null));
343                  } catch (Exception e) {
344                     actions.put(k, new BeanPropertyValue(bpm, k, null, e));
345                  }
346               });
347            }
348         });
349
350         actions.forEach((k, v) -> {
351            if (v.getThrown() != null || valueFilter.test(v.getValue()))
352               action.apply(v.getMeta(), v.getName(), v.getValue(), v.getThrown());
353         });
354      }
355
356      return this;
357   }
358
359   /**
360    * Gets a property on the bean.
361    *
362    * <p>
363    * If an {@code ObjectSwap}-style transform is associated with this bean property or bean property type class, then this method
364    * will return the transformed value.
365    * For example, if the bean property type class is a {@link Date} and the bean property has the
366    * {@code DateFormat.ISO_INSTANT} setting associated with it through the
367    * {@code @Swap(value)} annotation, this method will return a String containing an
368    * ISO8601 date-time string value.
369    *
370    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
371    * <p class='bjava'>
372    *    <jc>// Construct a bean with a 'birthDate' Date field</jc>
373    *    Person <jv>person</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Person();
374    *    <jv>person</jv>.setBirthDate(<jk>new</jk> Date(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6));
375    *
376    *    <jc>// Create a marshalling context and add the ISO8601 date-time swap</jc>
377    *    MarshallingContext <jv>marshallingContext</jv> = MarshallingContext.<jsm>create</jsm>().dateFormat(DateFormat.<jsf>ISO_INSTANT</jsf>).build();
378    *
379    *    <jc>// Wrap our bean in a bean map</jc>
380    *    BeanMap&lt;Person&gt; <jv>beanMap</jv> = <jv>marshallingContext</jv>.toBeanMap(<jv>person</jv>);
381    *
382    *    <jc>// Get the field as a string (i.e. "'1901-03-03T04:05:06-5000'")</jc>
383    *    String <jv>birthDate</jv> = <jv>beanMap</jv>.get(<js>"birthDate"</js>);
384    * </p>
385    *
386    * @param property The name of the property to get.
387    * @return The property value.
388    * @throws RuntimeException if any of the following occur.
389    *    <ol>
390    *       <li>BeanMapEntry does not exist on the underlying object.
391    *       <li>Security settings prevent access to the underlying object getter method.
392    *       <li>An exception occurred inside the getter method.
393    *    </ol>
394    */
395   @Override /* Overridden from Map */
396   public Object get(Object property) {
397      var pName = s(property);
398      var p = getPropertyMeta(pName);
399      if (p == null)
400         return meta.onReadProperty(this.bean, pName, null);
401      return p.get(this, pName);
402   }
403
404   /**
405    * Same as {@link #get(Object)} but casts the value to the specific type.
406    *
407    * @param <T2> The type to cast to.
408    * @param property The name of the property to get.
409    * @param c The type to cast to.
410    * @return The property value.
411    * @throws RuntimeException if any of the following occur.
412    *    <ol>
413    *       <li>BeanMapEntry does not exist on the underlying object.
414    *       <li>Security settings prevent access to the underlying object getter method.
415    *       <li>An exception occurred inside the getter method.
416    *    </ol>
417    * @throws ClassCastException if property is not the specified type.
418    */
419   @SuppressWarnings({
420      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T2 for property retrieval
421   })
422   public <T2> T2 get(String property, Class<T2> c) {
423      var pName = s(property);
424      var p = getPropertyMeta(pName);
425      if (p == null)
426         return (T2)meta.onReadProperty(this.bean, pName, null);
427      return (T2)p.get(this, pName);
428   }
429
430   /**
431    * Returns the wrapped bean object.
432    *
433    * <p>
434    * Triggers bean creation if bean has read-only properties set through a constructor defined by the
435    * {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation.
436    *
437    * <p>
438    * The post-creation Optional&lt;X&gt; initialization step (which seeds null {@link Optional} properties with
439    * {@link BeanInfo#getOptionalDefault()}) is skipped for properties built via the bean-modeling-only path
440    * ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}) because per-property type metadata is unavailable;
441    * those properties are left untouched and any {@link Optional}-typed field stays at its constructor-assigned
442    * value.
443    *
444    * @return The inner bean object.
445    */
446   public T getBean() {
447      T b = getBean(true);
448
449      // If we have any arrays that need to be constructed, do it now.
450      if (nn(arrayPropertyCache)) {
451         arrayPropertyCache.forEach((k, v) -> {
452            try {
453               getPropertyMeta(k).setArray(b, v);
454            } catch (Exception e1) {
455               throw toRex(e1);
456            }
457         });
458         arrayPropertyCache = null;
459      }
460
461      // Initialize any null Optional<X> fields.  Skip properties whose ClassMeta is unavailable
462      // (bean-modeling-only path — Optional handling is a marshalling concern that requires type metadata).
463      meta.getProperties().forEach((k,v) -> {
464         var cm = v.getBeanInfo();
465         if (nn(cm) && cm.isOptional() && v.get(this, k) == null)
466            v.set(this, k, cm.getOptionalDefault());
467      });
468
469      // Do the same for hidden fields.
470      meta.getHiddenProperties().forEach((k, v) -> {
471         var cm = v.getBeanInfo();
472         if (nn(cm) && cm.isOptional() && v.get(this, k) == null)
473            v.set(this, k, cm.getOptionalDefault());
474      });
475
476      return b;
477   }
478
479   /**
480    * Returns the wrapped bean object.
481    *
482    * <p>
483    * If <c>create</c> is <jk>false</jk>, then this method may return <jk>null</jk> if the bean has read-only
484    * properties set through a constructor defined by the {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation.
485    *
486    * <p>
487    * This method does NOT always return the bean in it's final state.
488    * Array properties temporary stored as ArrayLists are not finalized until the {@link #getBean()} method is called.
489    *
490    * @param create If bean hasn't been instantiated yet, then instantiate it.
491    * @return The inner bean object.
492    */
493   public T getBean(boolean create) {
494      /** If this is a read-only bean, then we need to create it. */
495      if (bean == null && create && ine(meta.getConstructorArgs())) {
496         var props = meta.getConstructorArgs();
497         var c = meta.getConstructor();
498         var args = new Object[props.size()];
499         for (var i = 0; i < props.size(); i++) {
500            var propName = props.get(i);
501            var rawVal = propertyCache.remove(propName);
502            // Convert value to expected property type if it's not already the correct type.
503            // Uses the same logic as MarshallingSession.convertToMemberType's early-return check:
504            // collections/maps with specific element/value types always need conversion.
505            if (rawVal != null) {
506               var pm = getPropertyMeta(propName);
507               if (pm != null) {
508                  var cm = pm.getBeanInfo();
509                  var needsConversion = !cm.inner().isInstance(rawVal)
510                     || (cm.isCollection() && !cm.getElementType().isObject())
511                     || (cm.isMap() && !cm.getValueType().is(Object.class));
512                  if (needsConversion)
513                     rawVal = session.convertToType(rawVal, cm);
514               }
515            }
516            args[i] = rawVal;
517         }
518         try {
519            bean = c.<T>newInstance(args);
520            propertyCache.forEach(this::put);
521            propertyCache = null;
522         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
523            throw brex(e, meta.getBeanInfo().inner(), "IllegalArgumentException occurred on call to class constructor ''{0}'' with argument types ''{1}''", c.getNameSimple(),
524               Arrays.toString(getClasses(args)));
525         } catch (Exception e) {
526            throw brex(e);
527         }
528      }
529      return bean;
530   }
531
532   /**
533    * Returns the bean session that created this bean map.
534    *
535    * <p>
536    * The returned value is the bean-modeling SPI seam.  Marshalling-side callers needing the concrete
537    * marshalling-session implementation can cast — every session wired in via
538    * {@link #setBeanSession(BeanSession)} on the marshalling-side path is currently a
539    * {@code MarshallingSession}.
540    *
541    * @return The bean session that created this bean map.
542    */
543   public final BeanSession getBeanSession() { return session; }
544
545   /**
546    * Returns the {@link BeanInfo} of the wrapped bean.
547    *
548    * @return The class type of the wrapped bean.
549    */
550   @Override /* Overridden from Delegate */
551   public BeanInfo<T> getBeanInfo() { return this.meta.getBeanInfo(); }
552
553   /**
554    * Returns the metadata associated with this bean map.
555    *
556    * @return The metadata associated with this bean map.
557    */
558   public BeanMeta<T> getMeta() { return meta; }
559
560   /**
561    * Extracts the specified field values from this bean and returns it as a simple Map.
562    *
563    * <p>
564    * The returned map is a <i>live</i> view over this bean map: each entry's {@code getValue} reads through to
565    * {@code BeanMap.get} and {@code setValue} writes through to {@code BeanMap.put}.  Unknown {@code fields}
566    * (keys not present in this bean map) are silently skipped.
567    *
568    * @param fields The fields to extract.
569    * @return
570    *    A new map with fields as key-value pairs.
571    *    <br>Note that modifying the values in this map will also modify the underlying bean.
572    */
573   public Map<String,Object> getProperties(String...fields) {
574      Map<String,Object> thisMap = this;
575      var entries = new ArrayList<Map.Entry<String,Object>>(fields.length);
576      for (var k : fields) {
577         if (thisMap.containsKey(k)) {
578            entries.add(new Map.Entry<>() {
579               @Override public String getKey() { return k; }
580               @Override public Object getValue() { return thisMap.get(k); }
581               @Override public Object setValue(Object v) { return thisMap.put(k, v); }
582            });
583         }
584      }
585      return new AbstractMap<>() {
586         @Override public Set<Map.Entry<String,Object>> entrySet() {
587            return new AbstractSet<>() {
588               @Override public Iterator<Map.Entry<String,Object>> iterator() { return entries.iterator(); }
589               @Override public int size() { return entries.size(); }
590            };
591         }
592      };
593   }
594
595   /**
596    * Returns the specified property on this bean map.
597    *
598    * <p>
599    * Allows you to get and set an individual property on a bean without having a handle to the bean itself by using
600    * the {@link BeanMapEntry#getValue()} and {@link BeanMapEntry#setValue(Object)} methods.
601    *
602    * <p>
603    * This method can also be used to get metadata on a property by calling the {@link BeanMapEntry#getMeta()} method.
604    *
605    * @param propertyName The name of the property to look up.
606    * @return The bean property, or null if the bean has no such property.
607    */
608   public BeanMapEntry getProperty(String propertyName) {
609      var p = getPropertyMeta(propertyName);
610      if (p == null)
611         return null;
612      return new BeanMapEntry(this, p, propertyName);
613   }
614
615   /**
616    * Returns the metadata on the specified property.
617    *
618    * <p>
619    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the property does not exist on the bean <em>or</em> if the property exists but is
620    * excluded by the current active view.  The latter allows the caller (e.g. {@link #put(String, Object)}) to
621    * route out-of-view input through the existing unknown/ignored-property path governed by
622    * {@link BeanConfigContext#isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties()}.
623    *
624    * @param propertyName The name of the bean property.
625    * @return Metadata on the specified property, or <jk>null</jk> if that property does not exist or is out of view.
626    */
627   public BeanPropertyMeta getPropertyMeta(String propertyName) {
628      var p = meta.getPropertyMeta(propertyName);
629      if (p != null && ! isInActiveView(p))
630         return null;
631      return p;
632   }
633
634   /**
635    * Same as {@link #get(Object)} except bypasses any {@code ObjectSwap} associated with the bean property or
636    * {@link BeanFilter} associated with the bean class.
637    *
638    * @param property The name of the property to get.
639    * @return The raw property value.
640    */
641   public Object getRaw(Object property) {
642      var pName = s(property);
643      var p = getPropertyMeta(pName);
644      if (p == null)
645         return null;
646      return p.getRaw(this, pName);
647   }
648
649   /**
650    * Returns the names of all properties associated with the bean.
651    *
652    * <p>
653    * The returned set is unmodifiable.
654    */
655   @Override /* Overridden from Map */
656   public Set<String> keySet() {
657      if (meta.getDynaProperty() == null)
658         return meta.getProperties().keySet();
659      Set<String> l = set();
660      meta.getProperties().forEach((k, v) -> {
661         if (! "*".equals(k))
662            l.add(k);
663      });
664      try {
665         l.addAll(meta.getDynaProperty().getDynaMap(bean).keySet());
666      } catch (Exception e) {
667         throw new BeanRuntimeException(e);
668      }
669      return l;
670   }
671
672   /**
673    * Convenience method for loading this map with the contents of the specified map.
674    *
675    * <p>
676    * Identical to {@link #putAll(Map)} except as a fluent-style method.
677    *
678    * @param entries The map containing the entries to add to this map.
679    * @return This object.
680    */
681   @SuppressWarnings({
682      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts for dynamic bean property access
683      "rawtypes", // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
684   })
685   public BeanMap<T> load(Map entries) {
686      putAll(entries);
687      return this;
688   }
689
690   /**
691    * Sets a property on the bean.
692    *
693    * <p>
694    * If there is a {@code ObjectSwap} associated with this bean property or bean property type class, then you must pass in a transformed value.
695    * For example, if the bean property type class is a {@link Date} and the bean property has the
696    * {@code DateFormat.ISO_INSTANT} setting associated with it through the
697    * {@code @Swap(value)} annotation, the value being passed in must be
698    * a String containing an ISO8601 date-time string value.
699    *
700    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
701    * <p class='bjava'>
702    *    <jc>// Construct a bean with a 'birthDate' Date field</jc>
703    *    Person <jv>person</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Person();
704    *
705    *    <jc>// Create a marshalling context and add the ISO8601 date-time swap</jc>
706    *    MarshallingContext <jv>marshallingContext</jv> = MarshallingContext.<jsm>create</jsm>().dateFormat(DateFormat.<jsf>ISO_INSTANT</jsf>).build();
707    *
708    *    <jc>// Wrap our bean in a bean map</jc>
709    *    BeanMap&lt;Person&gt; <jv>beanMap</jv> = <jv>marshallingContext</jv>.toBeanMap(<jv>person</jv>);
710    *
711    *    <jc>// Set the field</jc>
712    *    <jv>beanMap</jv>.put(<js>"birthDate"</js>, <js>"'1901-03-03T04:05:06-5000'"</js>);
713    * </p>
714    *
715    * @param property The name of the property to set.
716    * @param value The value to set the property to.
717    * @return
718    *    If the {@code beanMapPutReturnsOldValue} setting is <jk>true</jk> on the session's bean configuration,
719    *    then the old value of the property is returned.
720    *    Otherwise, this method always returns <jk>null</jk>.
721    * @throws
722    *    RuntimeException if any of the following occur.
723    *    <ul>
724    *       <li>BeanMapEntry does not exist on the underlying object.
725    *       <li>Security settings prevent access to the underlying object setter method.
726    *       <li>An exception occurred inside the setter method.
727    *    </ul>
728    */
729   @Override /* Overridden from Map */
730   public Object put(String property, Object value) {
731      var p = getPropertyMeta(property);
732      if (p == null) {
733         if (meta.getConfig().isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties() || property.equals(typePropertyName))
734            return meta.onWriteProperty(bean, property, null);
735
736         p = getPropertyMeta("*");
737         if (p == null)
738            throw brex(meta.getClassInfo(), "Bean property ''{0}'' not found.", property);
739      }
740      return p.set(this, property, value);
741   }
742
743   /**
744    * Given a string containing variables of the form <c>"{property}"</c>, replaces those variables with property
745    * values in this bean.
746    *
747    * @param s The string containing variables.
748    * @return A new string with variables replaced, or the same string if no variables were found.
749    */
750   public String resolveVars(String s) {
751      return formatNamed(s, this);
752   }
753
754   /**
755    * Returns a simple collection of properties for this bean map.
756    *
757    * @return A simple collection of properties for this bean map.
758    */
759   protected Collection<BeanPropertyMeta> getProperties() { return meta.getProperties().values(); }
760
761   @SuppressWarnings({
762      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T for bean assignment
763   })
764   void setBean(Object bean) { this.bean = (T)bean; }
765
766   /**
767    * Compares the specified object with this map for equality.
768    *
769    * <p>
770    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the given object is also a map and the two maps represent the same
771    * mappings. More formally, two maps <c>m1</c> and <c>m2</c> represent the same mappings if
772    * <c>m1.entrySet().equals(m2.entrySet())</c>.
773    *
774    * <p>
775    * This implementation compares the entry sets of the two maps.
776    *
777    * @param o Object to be compared for equality with this map.
778    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is equal to this map.
779    */
780   @Override
781   public boolean equals(Object o) {
782      return o instanceof Map<?,?> o2 && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> x.entrySet().equals(y.entrySet()));
783   }
784
785   /**
786    * Returns the hash code value for this map.
787    *
788    * <p>
789    * The hash code of a map is defined to be the sum of the hash codes of each entry in the map's
790    * <c>entrySet()</c> view. This ensures that <c>m1.equals(m2)</c> implies that
791    * <c>m1.hashCode()==m2.hashCode()</c> for any two maps <c>m1</c> and <c>m2</c>, as required
792    * by the general contract of {@link Object#hashCode()}.
793    *
794    * <p>
795    * This implementation computes the hash code from the entry set.
796    *
797    * @return The hash code value for this map.
798    */
799   @Override
800   public int hashCode() {
801      return entrySet().hashCode();
802   }
803}
